cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A need or desire that energizes and directs behavior toward a goal

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Maslow's hierarchy placing physiological needs at the base, then safety, belonging, esteem, and self-actualization at the top

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Abraham Maslow
cards.answer: Proposed the hierarchy of needs theory; described self-actualization as the highest human motivation

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The highest level of Maslow's hierarchy — realizing one's full potential

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Theory that behavior is motivated by a physiological need to reduce bodily tension (hunger, thirst)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A tension or arousal state that motivates organisms to satisfy a need

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The body's tendency to maintain a stable internal environment (temperature, blood sugar, etc.)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Theory that motivation is best at an intermediate level of arousal — too little or too much impairs performance

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Motivation driven by internal rewards (interest, enjoyment) rather than external rewards

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Motivation driven by external rewards or consequences (grades, money, praise)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The reduction of intrinsic motivation when external rewards are added to a previously enjoyable activity

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The hypothalamic area whose stimulation increases hunger and whose damage causes loss of appetite

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The hypothalamic area whose stimulation reduces hunger; damage causes overeating

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Hormone secreted by fat cells that signals the brain to reduce hunger when fat stores are sufficient

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The hormone that increases hunger; secreted by the stomach when food intake is low

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An eating disorder characterized by self-starvation and intense fear of gaining weight

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An eating disorder involving cycles of binge eating followed by purging (vomiting, laxatives)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A complex reaction involving physiological arousal, expressive behaviors, and conscious experience

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Theory that emotion follows physiological arousal — we feel afraid because our heart is racing, not the other way around

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Theory that the thalamus simultaneously sends signals to the cortex and body, so physiological arousal and emotion occur together

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Two-factor theory: emotion = physiological arousal + cognitive label for that arousal (Schachter and Singer)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Paul Ekman
cards.answer: Identified six universal basic emotions (happiness, sadness, anger, fear, disgust, surprise) recognized across cultures

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The hypothesis that facial expressions can influence the emotions we experience

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A stable set of psychological characteristics that influence how a person thinks, feels, and behaves

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Sigmund Freud
cards.answer: Developed psychoanalytic theory of personality; proposed id, ego, superego, unconscious mind, and defense mechanisms

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Freud's primitive, pleasure-seeking part of personality operating on the pleasure principle

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Freud's rational part of personality that mediates between id and superego on the reality principle

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Freud's moral conscience; internalized standards and guilt

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Unconscious mental strategies that protect the ego from anxiety by distorting reality

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defense mechanism: pushing threatening thoughts out of consciousness

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defense mechanism: attributing one's unacceptable feelings to someone else

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defense mechanism: channeling unacceptable impulses into socially acceptable activities

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defense mechanism: refusing to accept a painful reality

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defense mechanism: reverting to childlike behaviors under stress

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Defense mechanism: creating logical reasons to justify unacceptable behaviors or feelings

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Freud's stages of personality development: oral, anal, phallic, latency, genital

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Freud's phallic stage crisis: boy's unconscious desire for mother and rivalry with father

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Personality assessment technique presenting ambiguous stimuli to reveal unconscious thoughts and feelings

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Freud's projective test using inkblot images to reveal unconscious content

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Projective test where subjects describe what is happening in ambiguous pictures

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The five broad personality traits: Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Neuroticism

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Humanistic psychologist who emphasized unconditional positive regard and self-concept in personality

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Carl Rogers
cards.answer: Humanistic psychologist; developed person-centered therapy; emphasized self-concept, unconditional positive regard, and self-actualization

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Rogers's concept: accepting and valuing a person regardless of their behavior or flaws

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A person's thoughts and feelings about themselves; their self-image

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Bandura's theory that behavior is influenced by the interaction between personal factors, behavior, and environment

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A person's belief in their ability to succeed at specific tasks

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Albert Bandura
cards.answer: Social-cognitive personality theorist; studied observational learning, self-efficacy, and reciprocal determinism

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An objective, self-report personality questionnaire (MMPI) that measures traits and psychopathology

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The type A / type B personality distinction related to stress response and coronary heart disease risk
